title: Connection Refused by Server
shortDescription: The browser's connection attempt was actively refused by the target server.
fullDescription: ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED occurs when the browser tries to connect to a server but the server actively refuses the connection. This means the server received the connection request but has no service listening on the requested port. It can also indicate a firewall blocking the connection or the website's server being down.

causes.cause: Website server is down or not running
Firewall or antivirus blocking the connection

symptoms: Browser shows This site cannot be reached - ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED
Error occurs for a specific website but others work
Error occurs for all websites
Works in one browser but not another

fixSteps.title: Verify the website is up
fixSteps.instruction: Try accessing the website from a different device or use an online service like downforeveryoneorjustme.com to check if the site is down.

fixSteps.title: Clear browser cache and cookies
fixSteps.instruction: Open browser settings > Privacy > Clear browsing data. Select Cached images/files and Cookies. Restart the browser.

fixSteps.title: Disable proxy settings
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Windows Settings > Network & Internet > Proxy. Turn off Use a proxy server. Also check browser-specific proxy settings.

fixSteps.title: Temporarily disable firewall/antivirus
fixSteps.instruction: Disable Windows Firewall or third-party firewall temporarily and try again.

whenToCallPro: If the error occurs for all websites and persists after all fixes, there may be a deeper network or malware issue.

faq.q: Does this mean the website is hacked?
faq.a: No. It simply means the server is not accepting connections. It is usually a temporary server issue or a local network/firewall problem.

faq.q: Why does it work on my phone but not my computer?
faq.a: Your computer may have a firewall, proxy, or DNS setting that is blocking the connection. Your phone likely uses a different network path.
Yes. Some VPNs block certain ports or route traffic through servers that cannot reach the destination. Try disconnecting the VPN.

title: Connection Reset by Server or Network
shortDescription: The connection to the server was unexpectedly reset during communication.
fullDescription: ERR_CONNECTION_RESET occurs when an established connection between the browser and server is unexpectedly terminated (TCP RST). This can happen due to network issues, server overload, firewall interference, or VPN problems. Unlike CONNECTION_REFUSED, this error means the connection was initially accepted but then dropped.

causes.cause: Firewall or ISP interrupting the connection

symptoms: Page loading fails with connection reset
Error occurs intermittently
Large file downloads fail mid-transfer
Error appears on specific websites

fixSteps.title: Refresh the page
fixSteps.instruction: Press Ctrl+Shift+R for a hard refresh. Try again after a few minutes.

fixSteps.title: Reset Winsock and TCP/IP
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: netsh winsock reset && netsh int ip reset && ipconfig /flushdns. Restart computer.

fixSteps.title: Disable VPN/proxy
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect VPN and disable proxy settings temporarily.

fixSteps.title: Check firewall rules
fixSteps.instruction: Review Windows Firewall and third-party firewall rules. Ensure the browser is allowed through.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists for all websites after Winsock reset, there may be ISP-level or hardware issues.

faq.a: Yes. CONNECTION_REFUSED means the server rejected the initial connection. CONNECTION_RESET means the connection was accepted but then dropped mid-communication.
Yes. Some ISPs use deep packet inspection that can disrupt connections, especially to certain types of content.

faq.q: Why does this happen only for some sites?
faq.a: Some servers may be overloaded or have strict connection timeout policies. It can also be caused by geographic network routing issues.

title: Connection Timed Out
shortDescription: The browser waited too long for the server to respond and gave up.
fullDescription: ERR_CONNECTION_TIMED_OUT occurs when the browser's TCP connection attempt does not receive any response from the server within the timeout period. The server may be unreachable, overloaded, or the network path may be blocked.

causes.cause: Server is unreachable or extremely slow
DNS resolving to wrong or unreachable IP

symptoms: Browser shows connection timed out after long wait
Loading spinner runs for a long time then fails
Some websites load while others time out
Error occurs more on slow connections

fixSteps.title: Check internet connection
fixSteps.instruction: Test your internet speed and connectivity. Try other websites to confirm the connection works.

fixSteps.title: Flush DNS cache
fixSteps.instruction: Open Command Prompt: ipconfig /flushdns

fixSteps.title: Change DNS servers
fixSteps.instruction: Change to Google DNS (8.8.8.8) or Cloudflare (1.1.1.1) in network adapter settings.

fixSteps.title: Disable browser extensions
fixSteps.instruction: Disable all browser extensions and try again. Ad blockers and privacy extensions can sometimes cause timeouts.

whenToCallPro: If all websites time out, contact your ISP to check for service issues.

faq.a: Possibly. Check using a different device or network. If it loads elsewhere, the issue is on your end (DNS, firewall, or network).
Yes. Very slow connections may not complete the TCP handshake within the browser's timeout period, especially for distant servers.
Your DNS server may be returning an outdated or incorrect IP address. Using a public DNS like Google (8.8.8.8) ensures faster and more accurate resolution.

title: SSL/TLS Protocol Error
shortDescription: The browser could not establish a secure SSL/TLS connection with the server.
fullDescription: ERR_SSL_PROTOCOL_ERROR occurs when the browser and server cannot agree on an SSL/TLS protocol version or the SSL handshake fails. This can be caused by incorrect system time, outdated SSL/TLS configuration, or network equipment interfering with the SSL handshake.

causes.cause: Outdated browser or OS not supporting modern TLS

symptoms: Browser shows SSL protocol error on HTTPS sites
Error appears on many or all HTTPS websites
Works in incognito mode but not normal mode
Error started after antivirus installation

fixSteps.title: Check system date and time
fixSteps.instruction: Right-click clock > Adjust date/time. Ensure Set time automatically is on. Click Sync now.

fixSteps.title: Clear SSL state
fixSteps.instruction: Open Internet Options > Content > Clear SSL state.

fixSteps.title: Disable antivirus HTTPS scanning
fixSteps.instruction: In antivirus settings, find SSL/HTTPS scanning or Web Shield and disable it. Retry the website.

fixSteps.title: Enable TLS 1.2 and 1.3
fixSteps.instruction: Open Internet Options > Advanced. Scroll to Security section. Ensure TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 are checked.

whenToCallPro: If the error occurs only on a website you manage, the server's SSL configuration needs to be reviewed.

faq.q: Why does system time matter for SSL?
faq.a: SSL certificates have validity dates. If your system clock is wrong, the browser may think certificates are expired or not yet valid, causing protocol errors.
Yes. Antivirus programs with HTTPS scanning intercept SSL connections to inspect traffic. This can break the SSL handshake for some websites.
The error itself is the browser protecting you from an insecure connection. Do not bypass it unless you are certain the cause is benign.

title: SSL Version or Cipher Suite Mismatch
shortDescription: The browser and server could not agree on an SSL/TLS version or cipher suite.
fullDescription: This error occurs when the browser and web server have no common SSL/TLS protocol version or cipher suite. Modern browsers require at least TLS 1.2, and servers still using TLS 1.0/1.1 or weak ciphers will be rejected.

causes.cause: CDN or load balancer SSL misconfiguration

symptoms: Browser shows SSL version or cipher mismatch error
Error occurs only on specific older websites
Other modern websites work fine
Error appeared after browser or OS update

fixSteps.title: Try a different browser
fixSteps.instruction: Try accessing the site in a different browser. Some browsers have different TLS requirements.

fixSteps.title: Check TLS settings
fixSteps.instruction: Open Internet Options > Advanced > Security. Verify TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3 are enabled.

fixSteps.title: Contact website administrator
fixSteps.instruction: If you need to access the site, contact its administrator and request they update their SSL/TLS configuration.

fixSteps.title: Clear browser SSL cache
fixSteps.instruction: Open Internet Options > Content > Clear SSL state. Also clear browser cache.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the website, consult a web server administrator to update SSL/TLS configuration.

faq.q: Why did this suddenly start happening?
faq.a: Browsers regularly remove support for older TLS versions and weak ciphers. A recent browser update may have dropped support for the version the server uses.

faq.q: Can I force the browser to use older TLS?
faq.a: Some browsers allow enabling TLS 1.0/1.1 in settings, but this is not recommended as it weakens your security.

faq.q: Is it safe to bypass this error?
faq.a: Not recommended. The server's outdated encryption may be vulnerable to attacks.

title: Certificate Authority Not Trusted
shortDescription: The website's SSL certificate was issued by an authority that the browser does not trust.
fullDescription: This error occurs when the SSL certificate presented by the website was not issued by a trusted Certificate Authority (CA). This can happen with self-signed certificates, expired intermediate certificates, certificates from unknown CAs, or when a network proxy is performing SSL inspection.

causes.cause: Expired or missing intermediate certificate on server
Incorrect system date invalidating certificate chain

symptoms: Browser shows Your connection is not private warning
NET::ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID displayed
Certificate error with Advanced option to proceed
Error on corporate network but not on home network

fixSteps.title: Check system date and time
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure your system clock is correct. Incorrect time can cause certificate validation failures.

fixSteps.title: Check if it is a known site
fixSteps.instruction: If this is a well-known website, the issue is likely on your end. For unknown sites, do not proceed.

fixSteps.title: Install corporate root certificate
fixSteps.instruction: On corporate networks with SSL inspection, install the organization's root CA certificate from IT.

fixSteps.title: Disable antivirus HTTPS scanning
fixSteps.instruction: Some antivirus programs replace SSL certificates. Disable HTTPS/SSL scanning in antivirus settings.

whenToCallPro: If you are on a corporate network, contact IT to install the proper root certificate.

faq.q: Is it safe to click Proceed anyway?
faq.a: Only if you fully trust the website and understand why the certificate is not trusted. For unknown sites, never proceed.

faq.q: Why does this happen on my corporate network?
faq.a: Many corporations use SSL inspection proxies that replace website certificates with their own. This requires installing the corporate root CA certificate.
Yes. Antivirus programs with HTTPS scanning replace certificates, which browsers may not trust. Disabling HTTPS scanning usually fixes this.

title: SSL Certificate Date Invalid
shortDescription: The website's SSL certificate has expired or is not yet valid.
fullDescription: This error occurs when the SSL certificate's validity period does not include the current date. Either the certificate has expired, or the system clock is incorrect causing valid certificates to appear invalid.

causes.cause: System clock is incorrect (CMOS battery dead)
Website's SSL certificate has genuinely expired
Certificate not yet valid (server date issue)

symptoms: Certificate expired warning in browser
Error appears on multiple HTTPS websites
System clock shows incorrect date or time
Error appears on specific website only

fixSteps.title: Fix system date and time
fixSteps.instruction: Right-click clock > Adjust date/time. Enable Set time automatically and Set time zone automatically. Click Sync now.

fixSteps.title: Replace CMOS battery if clock keeps resetting
fixSteps.instruction: If the clock resets every time you power off, the CMOS battery needs replacement. It is a CR2032 coin cell on the motherboard.

fixSteps.title: Verify from another device
fixSteps.instruction: Check if the same website shows a certificate error on your phone or another computer.

fixSteps.title: Clear browser SSL cache
fixSteps.instruction: Internet Options > Content > Clear SSL state.

whenToCallPro: If the certificate is genuinely expired and you need to contact the website owner to renew it.

faq.q: Why does my clock keep resetting?
faq.a: The CMOS battery on the motherboard stores the clock when the PC is off. When it dies (every 5-10 years), the clock resets on each power-off.
An expired certificate does not provide less encryption, but it may indicate the website is not being maintained. Proceed with caution.

faq.q: Can a website renew its certificate?
faq.a: Yes. Website administrators can renew their SSL certificate. Many use auto-renewal through services like Let's Encrypt.

title: Domain Name Does Not Exist
shortDescription: The DNS server responded that the requested domain name does not exist.
fullDescription: DNS_PROBE_FINISHED_NXDOMAIN means the DNS server returned an NXDOMAIN response, indicating the domain name does not exist in the DNS system. This can be caused by a typo in the URL, an expired domain, stale DNS cache, or the DNS server being unable to resolve the name.

causes.cause: Domain name has expired or been deregistered
Local DNS cache contains stale or incorrect entries

symptoms: Browser shows This site can't be reached - DNS_PROBE_FINISHED_NXDOMAIN
Error for a specific domain while others work
Website was working before but now shows NXDOMAIN
Error occurs on one device but not others

fixSteps.title: Check the URL for typos
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the URL is spelled correctly. Try searching for the website name in a search engine.

fixSteps.title: Flush DNS cache
fixSteps.instruction: Open Command Prompt: ipconfig /flushdns. Also flush browser DNS: chrome://net-internals/#dns > Clear host cache.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS servers
fixSteps.instruction: Switch to public DNS: Google (8.8.8.8, 8.8.4.4) or Cloudflare (1.1.1.1, 1.0.0.1).

fixSteps.title: Check hosts file
fixSteps.instruction: Open C:\Windows\System32\drivers\etc\hosts in Notepad (as admin). Look for entries redirecting the domain.

whenToCallPro: If the domain genuinely does not exist (expired), you need to contact the domain owner.

faq.a: NXDOMAIN means Non-Existent Domain. The DNS server confirmed the domain name does not exist.

faq.q: Can my ISP's DNS cause this?
faq.a: Yes. ISP DNS servers can sometimes have issues. Switching to a public DNS like Google (8.8.8.8) often resolves it.

faq.q: Could malware modify my hosts file?
faq.a: Yes. Some malware adds entries to the hosts file to block security updates or redirect websites.

title: No Internet Connection for DNS Resolution
shortDescription: The browser cannot perform DNS resolution because there is no active internet connection.
fullDescription: DNS_PROBE_FINISHED_NO_INTERNET indicates that the browser cannot reach any DNS server because the network connection is down. No DNS response was received because the internet connection is not working.

causes.cause: Internet connection is down or disconnected
Wi-Fi disconnected or out of range

symptoms: No websites load at all
Wi-Fi shows connected but no internet
Network icon shows limited or no connectivity
Other devices on the network work fine

fixSteps.title: Check network connection
fixSteps.instruction: Click the network icon in the system tray. Verify you are connected to Wi-Fi or Ethernet.

fixSteps.title: Restart router and modem
fixSteps.instruction: Power off the router and modem for 30 seconds, then power them back on. Wait 2 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Reset network adapter
fixSteps.instruction: Go to Settings > Network & Internet > Status > Network reset.

fixSteps.title: Reset TCP/IP stack
fixSteps.instruction: Open elevated Command Prompt: netsh winsock reset && netsh int ip reset && ipconfig /release && ipconfig /renew. Restart.

whenToCallPro: If no devices can access the internet, contact your ISP.

faq.q: Why does Wi-Fi say connected but there is no internet?
faq.a: Connected means your device is linked to the router. The router itself may not have an internet connection, or DHCP may not have assigned an IP properly.

faq.q: Will restarting the router fix it?
faq.a: Often yes. Routers can accumulate errors and restarting clears their memory and re-establishes the ISP connection.
Similar but slightly different. DNS_PROBE_FINISHED_NO_INTERNET specifically indicates DNS cannot resolve. ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED indicates no network at all.

title: Network Connection Changed
shortDescription: The network connection changed while the page was loading, interrupting the request.
fullDescription: ERR_NETWORK_CHANGED occurs when the browser detects that the network interface changed during a page load. This commonly happens when switching between Wi-Fi networks, connecting/disconnecting VPN, or when the IP address changes.

causes.cause: Unstable Wi-Fi connection dropping and reconnecting

symptoms: Page loading fails with network changed error
Error occurs when VPN connects or disconnects
Error during Wi-Fi handoff between access points
Error appears intermittently

fixSteps.title: Refresh the page
fixSteps.instruction: Simply press F5 or click refresh. The page should load on the new network.

fixSteps.title: Ensure stable network connection
fixSteps.instruction: Wait for the network transition to complete, then retry.

fixSteps.title: Disable Wi-Fi auto-switching
fixSteps.instruction: In Wi-Fi settings, forget weak networks. Set your preferred network to Connect automatically.

fixSteps.title: Flush DNS and sockets
fixSteps.instruction: In Chrome: chrome://net-internals/#sockets > Close idle sockets, and chrome://net-internals/#dns > Clear host cache.

whenToCallPro: This error is almost always transient and does not require professional help.

faq.a: No. It is a transient error caused by a network change. Simply refreshing the page almost always fixes it.
VPNs change your network routing and IP address. Active connections are disrupted during the switch.
Rarely, but it can happen if the ethernet cable is momentarily disconnected or if the IP address changes via DHCP.

title: Internet Disconnected
shortDescription: The computer is not connected to any network, preventing all internet access.
fullDescription: ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED indicates that the browser detected no active network connection at all. The computer is not connected to any Wi-Fi or ethernet network.
symptoms: No internet access from any application
Network icon shows disconnected
Chrome shows the dinosaur game
Wi-Fi list is empty or shows no networks

fixSteps.title: Check physical connection
fixSteps.instruction: For Wi-Fi: ensure Wi-Fi is turned on. For Ethernet: check cable is plugged in at both ends.

fixSteps.title: Disable airplane mode
fixSteps.instruction: Click the network icon in system tray. Ensure Airplane mode is off.

fixSteps.title: Enable network adapter
fixSteps.instruction: Device Manager > Network adapters. If disabled (down arrow), right-click > Enable.

fixSteps.title: Restart network adapter
fixSteps.instruction: Settings > Network & Internet > Wi-Fi or Ethernet. Toggle the adapter off and back on.

whenToCallPro: If the network adapter does not appear in Device Manager, it may be a hardware failure.

faq.q: Why does my Wi-Fi keep disconnecting?
faq.a: Common causes include outdated Wi-Fi drivers, power management settings disabling the adapter, or distance from the router.

faq.q: Can a Windows update disable my network?
faq.a: Yes. Some updates replace network drivers, which can cause the adapter to stop working. Update the driver from the manufacturer.
Chrome's offline dinosaur game appears when there is no internet. You can play it by pressing the spacebar.

title: Server Name Could Not Be Resolved
shortDescription: The browser could not resolve the website's hostname to an IP address through DNS.
fullDescription: ERR_NAME_NOT_RESOLVED indicates that DNS resolution failed for the requested hostname. This is similar to DNS_PROBE_FINISHED_NXDOMAIN but is more generic - it can occur due to DNS server issues, network configuration problems, or corrupted DNS cache.
symptoms: Cannot access websites by name but can by IP address
Some websites work while others fail
Error occurs on one device but not others
Error started after DNS or network change

fixSteps.title: Flush DNS cache
fixSteps.instruction: Open Command Prompt: ipconfig /flushdns. In Chrome: chrome://net-internals/#dns > Clear host cache.

fixSteps.title: Change DNS servers
fixSteps.instruction: Set DNS to Google (8.8.8.8) or Cloudflare (1.1.1.1) in network adapter settings.

fixSteps.title: Reset network stack
fixSteps.instruction: netsh winsock reset && netsh int ip reset && ipconfig /flushdns && ipconfig /release && ipconfig /renew

fixSteps.title: Disable DNS prefetching
fixSteps.instruction: In Chrome settings: Settings > Privacy > Use prediction service > Off.

whenToCallPro: If the issue persists after DNS changes and affects all devices, contact your ISP.

faq.q: How can I test if DNS is working?
faq.a: Open Command Prompt and type: nslookup google.com. If it returns an IP address, DNS is working.

faq.q: What DNS server should I use?
faq.a: Google DNS (8.8.8.8) and Cloudflare DNS (1.1.1.1) are reliable, fast, and free public DNS services.

faq.q: Can my hosts file cause this?
faq.a: If the hosts file maps the domain to a non-existent IP, it can cause resolution failures.

title: Cache Miss - Form Resubmission Required
shortDescription: The page requires form data resubmission that the browser cache no longer has.
fullDescription: ERR_CACHE_MISS occurs when the browser tries to re-display a page that was the result of a form submission (POST request), but the form data is no longer in the cache. This commonly happens when using the Back button after submitting a form.

causes.cause: Using Back button after form submission

symptoms: Error when pressing Back button after form submission
Page cannot be displayed with cache miss error
Error on confirmation pages
Refresh required to view the page

fixSteps.title: Refresh the page
fixSteps.instruction: Press F5 to reload. If prompted about resubmitting form data, click Continue.

fixSteps.title: Navigate directly
fixSteps.instruction: Instead of using Back, navigate directly to the page using its URL.

fixSteps.title: Clear browser cache
fixSteps.instruction: Clear browser cache and cookies, then navigate to the page fresh.

fixSteps.title: Disable cache-disabling extensions
fixSteps.instruction: Some developer extensions disable caching. Check and disable them.

whenToCallPro: This error does not require professional help.

faq.q: Did I lose my form submission?
faq.a: No. The form was already submitted. The error just means the browser cannot display the submission page again from cache.
Partially. Well-designed websites use Post/Redirect/Get pattern to avoid this issue.
Navigate forward instead of using the Back button after form submissions.

title: Too Many Redirects
shortDescription: The page redirected too many times, indicating a redirect loop.
fullDescription: ERR_TOO_MANY_REDIRECTS occurs when the browser follows a chain of HTTP redirects that exceeds the maximum allowed. This indicates a redirect loop and is almost always a server-side configuration issue.

causes.cause: CDN or caching service redirect misconfiguration

symptoms: Browser shows too many redirects error
Page never loads, keeps redirecting
Error for specific website while others work
Error appeared after website update

fixSteps.title: Clear cookies for the site
fixSteps.instruction: Click the lock/info icon in the address bar > Cookies > Remove cookies for this site. Refresh.

fixSteps.title: Try incognito/private mode
fixSteps.instruction: Open the page in incognito or private browsing mode.

fixSteps.title: Clear all browser data
fixSteps.instruction: Clear all cookies, cache, and site data from browser settings.

fixSteps.title: Disable browser extensions
fixSteps.instruction: Disable all extensions, especially redirect or privacy extensions.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the website, check your redirect rules and CDN configuration.

faq.a: Usually not. It is typically a server-side misconfiguration. Clearing cookies may fix it if the loop is cookie-dependent.
Some redirect loops are caused by cookie-based authentication checks. Clearing corrupted cookies breaks the loop.

faq.q: Can I access the site another way?
faq.a: Try a different URL on the same site, or use a cached version from a search engine.

title: Bad Request
shortDescription: The server could not understand the request due to invalid syntax or malformed data.
fullDescription: HTTP 400 Bad Request indicates that the server cannot process the request because it is malformed. This can be caused by corrupted cookies, oversized request headers, invalid URL characters, or browser cache issues.

causes.cause: Corrupted or oversized cookies for the site

symptoms: Browser shows 400 Bad Request error page
Error occurs on specific site while others work
Works in incognito but not normal mode
Error started suddenly after previously working

fixSteps.title: Clear cookies for the site
fixSteps.instruction: Click the address bar lock icon > Cookies > Remove. Or browser settings > Cookies > search for the site > Delete.

fixSteps.title: Clear browser cache
fixSteps.instruction: Clear cached images and files from browser settings.

fixSteps.title: Check the URL
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the URL does not contain invalid characters, extra spaces, or encoding errors.

fixSteps.title: Try incognito mode
fixSteps.instruction: Open the page in incognito/private mode to test without cookies or cache.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the website and the error is caused by server-side configuration.

faq.a: When too many or too large cookies accumulate, the request header size can exceed the server's limit, causing a 400 error.
No. A 400 error is the server saying it did not understand the request.
Incognito mode uses no existing cookies or cache, sending a clean request.

title: Authentication Required
shortDescription: The server requires authentication credentials that were not provided or are invalid.
fullDescription: HTTP 401 Unauthorized indicates that the request lacks valid authentication credentials. The server is requesting that the client authenticate itself. If credentials are rejected, the username or password is incorrect.
symptoms: Browser shows login dialog or 401 error page
Previously accessible page now requires login
Login keeps failing despite correct credentials
Error after session timeout

fixSteps.title: Enter correct credentials
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your username and password are correct. Check for caps lock.

fixSteps.title: Clear saved passwords
fixSteps.instruction: Browser settings > Passwords. Find the site and delete saved credentials. Re-enter them.

fixSteps.title: Clear cookies and session data
fixSteps.instruction: Clear cookies for the specific site to reset the authentication session.

fixSteps.title: Try password reset
fixSteps.instruction: Use the site's Forgot password feature if credentials are forgotten.

whenToCallPro: If the 401 error occurs on a server you manage, check the authentication configuration.

faq.q: What is the difference between 401 and 403?
faq.a: 401 means you have not authenticated (not logged in). 403 means you are authenticated but do not have permission.
Yes. If the browser sends outdated saved credentials, the server may reject them.
Possibly. Multiple failed login attempts may lock your account. Contact the site's support.

title: Access Forbidden
shortDescription: The server understood the request but refuses to authorize it.
fullDescription: HTTP 403 Forbidden indicates that the server understands the request but refuses to grant access. Unlike 401, authentication will not help - the user does not have permission. This can be due to IP blocking, geographic restrictions, or access control lists.

causes.cause: Insufficient permissions for the requested resource
IP address blocked by server firewall
Directory listing disabled on web server

symptoms: Browser shows 403 Forbidden error
Page was accessible before but now shows 403
Error on specific page while other pages work
Error only from certain networks or locations

fixSteps.title: Clear browser cache and cookies
fixSteps.instruction: Clear cache and cookies for the site, then try again.

fixSteps.title: Check URL and permissions
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the URL is correct and whether you need to be logged in.

fixSteps.title: Try from different network
fixSteps.instruction: If your IP might be blocked, try from a different network.

fixSteps.title: Contact website administrator
fixSteps.instruction: If you should have access, contact the website's support.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the server, check file permissions, .htaccess rules, and firewall settings.

faq.a: Common reasons include IP blacklisting, geographic restrictions, or not having the required user role.
If the 403 is caused by IP blocking or geo-restriction, a VPN may help.

faq.q: Does 403 mean I did something wrong?
faq.a: Not necessarily. It can be triggered by automated security systems mistaking normal traffic for an attack.

title: Page Not Found
shortDescription: The server could not find the requested page or resource.
fullDescription: HTTP 404 Not Found indicates that the server is working but the specific page requested does not exist at the given URL. This can be caused by a typo, a deleted page, a changed URL structure, or a broken link.

causes.cause: Page was deleted or moved to a different URL

symptoms: Browser shows 404 Not Found page
Link from search results leads to 404
Bookmarked page no longer works
Custom 404 error page from the website

fixSteps.title: Check URL for typos
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully review the URL for misspellings.

fixSteps.title: Navigate to the site homepage
fixSteps.instruction: Go to the homepage and navigate from there. The URL structure may have changed.

fixSteps.title: Search for the content
fixSteps.instruction: Use the website's search or Google with site:example.com to find where the content moved.

fixSteps.title: Check web archive
fixSteps.instruction: Try web.archive.org to see a cached version if the page was permanently removed.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the website, set up proper 301 redirects for moved pages.

faq.a: No. A 404 means the server is working but the specific page does not exist. Other pages may work fine.

faq.q: Can I access the old page somehow?
faq.a: Try the Wayback Machine (web.archive.org) or Google's cached version.
A soft 404 is when a page returns 200 (OK) status but displays a not found message.

title: HTTP Method Not Allowed
shortDescription: The server does not support the HTTP method used for the target resource.
fullDescription: HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed indicates that the HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) used in the request is not supported by the target resource.

causes.cause: Form submitted with wrong method (GET vs POST)
API endpoint called with incorrect method
Security policy blocking certain HTTP methods

symptoms: Browser shows 405 Method Not Allowed
Form submission fails
API calls return 405
Works with one method but not another

fixSteps.title: Try accessing the page directly
fixSteps.instruction: Navigate to the URL directly in the browser address bar.

fixSteps.title: Clear cache and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Clear browser cache and cookies for the site.

fixSteps.title: Try a different browser
fixSteps.instruction: Some browsers handle HTTP methods differently.

fixSteps.title: Contact website support
fixSteps.instruction: Report the issue to the website as it is likely a server configuration issue.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the server, check the web server configuration and allowed methods.

faq.a: HTTP methods define the action: GET (retrieve), POST (submit), PUT (update), DELETE (remove). Servers can restrict which methods each endpoint accepts.

faq.q: Can I fix this as a user?
faq.a: Usually not, since it is a server-side configuration issue.
Not exactly. It is the server enforcing its API contract.

title: Request Timeout
shortDescription: The server timed out waiting for the client to send the complete request.
fullDescription: HTTP 408 Request Timeout indicates that the server waited too long for the client to send the complete request and closed the connection. The connection was established but the request data was not sent in time.
symptoms: Page load fails with 408 error
Large uploads fail consistently
Error occurs on slow connections
Error appears after long wait

fixSteps.title: Retry the request
fixSteps.instruction: Refresh the page or resubmit the form.

fixSteps.title: Check internet speed
fixSteps.instruction: Run a speed test. Low upload speed can cause timeouts.

fixSteps.title: Try a wired connection
fixSteps.instruction: Switch from Wi-Fi to ethernet for more reliable uploads.

fixSteps.title: Reduce upload size
fixSteps.instruction: Compress large files before uploading.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the server and need to increase request timeout.

faq.q: Is this the same as a connection timeout?
faq.a: No. Connection timeout means the initial connection failed. HTTP 408 means the connection was established but the request was sent too slowly.
Yes. Forms with large file uploads may take longer than the server allows.
Often yes, if the bottleneck is upload speed.

title: Rate Limit Exceeded
shortDescription: The server is rejecting requests because the client has sent too many in a given time period.
fullDescription: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests indicates rate-limiting by the server. Too many requests were sent within a specific time window. This is a server-side protection mechanism against abuse.

causes.cause: Too many page refreshes or requests in short time
Automated scripts or bots on the computer
Browser extensions making excessive API calls
Shared IP address (many users behind same IP)

symptoms: Website returns 429 Too Many Requests
Access blocked after rapid page loading
API calls fail with rate limit error
Error includes Retry-After header

fixSteps.title: Wait and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Wait a few minutes before trying again.

fixSteps.title: Reduce request frequency
fixSteps.instruction: Avoid rapidly refreshing or clicking through many pages quickly.

fixSteps.title: Check for automated scripts
fixSteps.instruction: Check if browser extensions are making excessive requests.

fixSteps.title: Try a different network
fixSteps.instruction: If on a shared network, try from a different IP.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the server and need to adjust rate limiting rules.

faq.q: How long do I have to wait?
faq.a: Check the Retry-After header. If not present, 1-5 minutes is usually sufficient.

faq.q: Can my IP get permanently blocked?
faq.a: Usually not. Most rate limits are temporary.

faq.q: Can other people on my network cause this?
faq.a: Yes. If you share a public IP, other users' requests count toward the same rate limit.

title: Internal Server Error
shortDescription: The server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request.
fullDescription: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error is a generic server-side error indicating something went wrong on the server. It can be caused by software bugs, configuration errors, resource exhaustion, or database failures.

causes.cause: Server overloaded or out of resources

symptoms: Generic 500 Internal Server Error page
Error occurs intermittently
Specific actions trigger the error
Works at some times but not others

fixSteps.title: Refresh the page
fixSteps.instruction: Press F5. If intermittent, it may work on retry.

fixSteps.title: Wait and try later
fixSteps.instruction: If overloaded, wait 5-15 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Clear browser cache
fixSteps.instruction: Clear cache and cookies for the site.

fixSteps.title: Check server status
fixSteps.instruction: Look for the website's status page or social media for outage announcements.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the server, check server logs for the specific error.

faq.a: Almost never. 500 errors are server-side problems.
If it persists, yes. The website owner may not be aware.
Application bugs, database failures, and server resource exhaustion are the most common causes.

title: Bad Gateway
shortDescription: A gateway or proxy server received an invalid response from an upstream server.
fullDescription: HTTP 502 Bad Gateway indicates that a server acting as a gateway or proxy received an invalid response from the upstream server it was trying to reach. This commonly occurs when a load balancer or CDN cannot get a valid response from the backend server.

causes.cause: Backend server is down or crashed
CDN or load balancer cannot reach origin
Network issue between proxy and backend

symptoms: Browser shows 502 Bad Gateway
Error from CDN error page (Cloudflare, AWS)
Website was working and suddenly shows 502
Error intermittent as backend servers restart

fixSteps.title: Refresh after a moment
fixSteps.instruction: Wait 30 seconds and refresh. The backend may recover quickly.

fixSteps.title: Try a different device
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the error occurs on other devices too, confirming server issue.

fixSteps.title: Clear DNS cache
fixSteps.instruction: ipconfig /flushdns in case DNS is pointing to a downed server.

fixSteps.title: Check status pages
fixSteps.instruction: Check downdetector.com for reported outages.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the infrastructure, check backend server health.

faq.a: A gateway or reverse proxy is an intermediary server that forwards requests to backend servers. Examples include Nginx, Cloudflare, and AWS ALB.

faq.q: Why does this happen on Cloudflare sites?
faq.a: When Cloudflare cannot reach the origin server, it shows its own 502 page. The issue is between Cloudflare and the origin.

faq.q: Can I do anything to fix it?
faq.a: As a user, no. Wait for the backend to recover or contact the website operator.

title: Service Unavailable
shortDescription: The server is temporarily unable to handle the request, usually due to maintenance or overload.
fullDescription: HTTP 503 Service Unavailable indicates that the server is temporarily unable to process requests. This is typically planned maintenance or unplanned overload. The server may include a Retry-After header.

causes.cause: Server overloaded with too many requests
Backend application pool stopped or crashed

symptoms: Browser shows 503 Service Unavailable
Maintenance page displayed
Error occurs during high-traffic events
Retry-After header present

fixSteps.title: Wait and retry
fixSteps.instruction: Check the Retry-After header or maintenance notice. Wait and try again.

fixSteps.title: Check for maintenance announcements
fixSteps.instruction: Check the website's social media or status page.

fixSteps.title: Try a cached version
fixSteps.instruction: Search for the page on Google and click the cached version.

fixSteps.title: Check downdetector
fixSteps.instruction: Visit downdetector.com to see if others report the same issue.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the server, check application pool status and server resources.

faq.a: Check the Retry-After header or maintenance notice. Typical windows range from minutes to hours.
Possibly, but more commonly it is overloaded or under maintenance.

faq.q: Can I speed up the fix?
faq.a: As a user, no. Wait for the server owner to address it.

title: Gateway Timeout
shortDescription: A gateway or proxy server did not receive a timely response from the upstream server.
fullDescription: HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout occurs when a gateway or proxy server did not receive a response from the upstream server within its timeout period. The backend server may be overloaded, unresponsive, or the network between gateway and backend may be slow.

causes.cause: Backend server processing request too slowly
Network latency between proxy and backend

symptoms: Browser shows 504 Gateway Timeout
Occurs on complex or data-heavy pages
Error during long-running operations
Intermittent depending on server load

fixSteps.title: Retry after a moment
fixSteps.instruction: Wait a minute and try again.

fixSteps.title: Try during off-peak hours
fixSteps.instruction: Access the site when there is less load.

fixSteps.title: Simplify your request
fixSteps.instruction: If the error occurs during complex operations, try a smaller scope.

fixSteps.title: Report to website
fixSteps.instruction: If consistent, report to the website operator.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the server, check backend performance and increase gateway timeout.

faq.a: 502 means the gateway got an invalid response. 504 means it got no response at all within the timeout period.

faq.q: Why does this happen with large reports?
faq.a: Generating large reports can take longer than the gateway's timeout allows.
As a user, no. The server administrator needs to increase the timeout.

title: QUIC Protocol Error
shortDescription: The QUIC (HTTP/3) protocol connection to the server failed.
fullDescription: ERR_QUIC_PROTOCOL_ERROR occurs when Chrome or Edge's QUIC (Quick UDP Internet Connections) protocol implementation encounters an error. QUIC is a modern transport protocol used for HTTP/3. Some networks or firewalls block QUIC traffic since it uses UDP instead of TCP.
symptoms: Page fails to load with QUIC protocol error
Error only on certain networks (works on others)
Google services intermittently fail
Error disappears when QUIC is disabled

fixSteps.title: Disable QUIC in Chrome
fixSteps.instruction: Open chrome://flags/#enable-quic, set Experimental QUIC protocol to Disabled. Restart Chrome.

fixSteps.title: Disable QUIC in Edge
fixSteps.instruction: Open edge://flags/#enable-quic, set to Disabled. Restart Edge.

fixSteps.title: Check firewall UDP rules
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure your firewall allows outbound UDP traffic on port 443.

fixSteps.title: Clear browser cache
fixSteps.instruction: Clear browser cache and DNS: chrome://net-internals/#dns > Clear, then chrome://net-internals/#sockets > Flush.

whenToCallPro: If your corporate network needs QUIC configured, contact IT.

faq.a: QUIC is a modern transport protocol developed by Google. It uses UDP instead of TCP and provides faster connections with built-in encryption. It is the foundation of HTTP/3.
Yes. Disabling QUIC forces the browser to use TCP/TLS (HTTP/2), which is fully supported everywhere. You may lose some speed but gain compatibility.

faq.q: Why do some networks block QUIC?
faq.a: Some enterprise firewalls block UDP port 443 because they cannot inspect QUIC traffic the way they inspect TCP/TLS traffic.

title: Response Blocked by Browser Security
shortDescription: The browser blocked the server's response due to security headers or CORS policy.
fullDescription: ERR_BLOCKED_BY_RESPONSE occurs when the browser blocks a server response due to security policies. This is commonly caused by Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) restrictions, Content Security Policy (CSP) headers, or X-Frame-Options preventing the page from being embedded in an iframe.
symptoms: Page or resource fails to load with blocked error
Embedded content (iframes) shows blank
API calls from web page fail silently
Console shows CORS or CSP violation messages

fixSteps.title: Check browser console for details
fixSteps.instruction: Press F12 > Console tab. Look for CORS, CSP, or X-Frame-Options error messages that explain the specific block.

fixSteps.title: Try accessing the resource directly
fixSteps.instruction: Copy the blocked resource URL and try loading it directly in a new tab.

fixSteps.title: Disable extensions
fixSteps.instruction: Some extensions modify security headers. Disable all extensions and test.

fixSteps.title: Contact website developer
fixSteps.instruction: This is typically a server-side configuration issue. Report it to the website developer.

whenToCallPro: If you manage the website, review your CORS, CSP, and security header configuration.

faq.a: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing is a security mechanism that controls which websites can make requests to other websites. Browsers enforce CORS to prevent malicious cross-site requests.

faq.q: Can I bypass this as a user?
faq.a: No, and you should not try. These security headers exist to protect you. If a legitimate site has misconfigured headers, contact its developer.

faq.q: Why does this only affect some browsers?
faq.a: Different browsers may enforce security headers slightly differently, and extensions can modify headers.

title: Browser Ran Out of Resources
shortDescription: The browser does not have enough memory or system resources to complete the request.
fullDescription: ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES occurs when the browser runs out of available memory or system resources to process a page or request. This can happen with too many tabs open, memory-heavy web applications, or when the system itself is low on RAM.

causes.cause: Too many browser tabs open consuming RAM
Memory leak in browser or extension

symptoms: Pages fail to load or crash with out of memory
Browser becomes very slow before error
New tabs fail to open
Task Manager shows browser using large amounts of RAM

fixSteps.title: Close unnecessary tabs
fixSteps.instruction: Close tabs you are not actively using. Each tab consumes 50-300MB of RAM.

fixSteps.title: Restart the browser
fixSteps.instruction: Close and reopen the browser to free all consumed memory.

fixSteps.title: Disable heavy extensions
fixSteps.instruction: Check Task Manager (Shift+Esc in Chrome) to see which extensions use the most memory. Disable them.

fixSteps.title: Close other applications
fixSteps.instruction: Close other running applications to free system RAM for the browser.

whenToCallPro: If the problem persists with few tabs, consider upgrading your system RAM.

faq.q: How much RAM does a browser need?
faq.a: Modern browsers need at least 4GB of system RAM for comfortable browsing. Heavy usage with many tabs may require 8GB or more.

faq.q: Why does Chrome use so much memory?
faq.a: Chrome runs each tab in a separate process for stability and security. This uses more memory but prevents one tab crash from affecting others.

faq.q: Can I limit browser memory usage?
faq.a: Chrome has a Memory Saver feature (Settings > Performance) that puts inactive tabs to sleep, freeing RAM.

title: Address Unreachable
shortDescription: The IP address of the website is unreachable from your network.
fullDescription: ERR_ADDRESS_UNREACHABLE indicates that the browser resolved the hostname to an IP address via DNS, but the IP address itself is unreachable. This means DNS worked but the actual server cannot be contacted, often due to routing issues, server being offline, or network-level blocks.
symptoms: Browser shows address unreachable error
DNS resolves but connection fails
Traceroute shows packets stopping at a hop
Works from other networks but not current one

fixSteps.title: Flush DNS and retry
fixSteps.instruction: ipconfig /flushdns. The DNS cache may have an old IP. Try again.

fixSteps.title: Try different DNS server
fixSteps.instruction: Switch to Google (8.8.8.8) or Cloudflare (1.1.1.1) DNS.

fixSteps.title: Check from different network
fixSteps.instruction: Try the site from mobile data or a different Wi-Fi to isolate the issue.

fixSteps.title: Run traceroute
fixSteps.instruction: Open Command Prompt: tracert example.com to see where the connection stops.

whenToCallPro: If the site is unreachable from all networks, the server is likely down. Contact the website operator.

faq.a: Yes. NAME_NOT_RESOLVED means DNS failed. ADDRESS_UNREACHABLE means DNS worked but the resulting IP address cannot be reached.

faq.q: Can my ISP block specific IPs?
faq.a: Yes. Some ISPs block certain IP addresses, especially those associated with geo-restricted or blocked content.
Traceroute shows each network hop between you and the destination. If it stops before reaching the server, there is a routing or firewall issue at that hop.

title: HTTP/2 Protocol Error
shortDescription: The browser encountered a protocol-level error in the HTTP/2 connection to the server.
fullDescription: ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR occurs when there is an issue with the HTTP/2 protocol communication between the browser and server. HTTP/2 is the modern version of HTTP used by most websites. Protocol errors can be caused by network interference, proxy issues, or server misconfiguration.

causes.cause: Network proxy or firewall corrupting HTTP/2 frames
Outdated network equipment not supporting HTTP/2

symptoms: Pages fail to load with HTTP/2 protocol error
Error occurs intermittently on various sites
Works in incognito or other browsers
Large pages or resources fail more often

fixSteps.title: Clear browser data
fixSteps.instruction: Clear cache, cookies, and site data. Close and reopen the browser.

fixSteps.title: Disable HTTP/2 temporarily
fixSteps.instruction: In Chrome: chrome://flags/#enable-spdy4 > set to Disabled. In Edge: edge://flags. Restart browser.

fixSteps.title: Disable antivirus HTTPS scanning
fixSteps.instruction: Disable SSL/HTTPS inspection in your antivirus to stop it from interfering with HTTP/2.

fixSteps.title: Try without VPN/proxy
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect any VPN or proxy and test the connection directly.

whenToCallPro: If disabling HTTP/2 resolves the issue, the root cause is likely a network appliance. Contact your network admin.

faq.a: HTTP/2 is the current standard web protocol, replacing HTTP/1.1. It supports multiplexing (multiple requests over one connection), header compression, and server push for faster loading.
Disabling HTTP/2 forces HTTP/1.1, which is slower but universally compatible. Use it as a temporary workaround while investigating the root cause.
Yes. HTTPS inspection by antivirus inserts itself between browser and server. If it does not properly support HTTP/2, it can corrupt the protocol frames.
